governance_bootstrap.summary: Customer-facing changes require exact verification evidence, deployment through origin/main, and no completion claim before a real entrypoint check.
OpenClaw runs on the Mac Mini; customer-facing code requires completion evidence and a real production verification.

verification.name: OpenClaw response-quality unit and regression suite
verification.result: 78 tests passed, including conclusion-first delivery, secret redaction, rendering-artifact blocking, and explicit-markup preservation.

verification.name: Slack delivery and E2E-probe regression suites
verification.target: OpenClaw Slack listener and E2E script
verification.command: .venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_slack_listener.py'; .venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_openclaw_slack_e2e_probe.py'
verification.result: 4 tests passed. CEO user-token probe acceptance and final-answer selection remain guarded.

verification.name: OpenClaw runtime entrypoint smoke test
verification.result: Pass. The actual OpenClaw run entrypoint returned the deterministic current-time response.

residual_risk: Mac Mini runtime verification and independent Red Team review are pending this change.
The shared delivery guard does not replace the planned typed request/evidence verifier for every tool route.
